title: Fix reconciliation Recent Runs table layout — absolute positioning and column truncation
analysis:
Two layout bugs in the recon panel's right column (Recent Runs table):

1. **Absolute positioning (lines 110-111):** The right column wrapper uses `min-h-[400px] lg:relative` and the inner card uses `lg:absolute lg:inset-0`. This takes the card out of document flow on large screens, causing scroll/overlap issues when the left and right column heights differ. The `lg:absolute lg:inset-0` pattern is not used anywhere else in the dashboard — other panels (performance.html, orchestrators.html) use natural grid flow.

2. **Header truncation:** The table headers ('When', 'Detail') get truncated at the right edge on 1440px viewports because the table uses `w-full` without preventing header text wrapping. The constrained absolute-positioned container exacerbates this.

**Fix approach:**
- Remove `lg:relative` and `min-h-[400px]` from the wrapper div (line 110).
- Replace `lg:absolute lg:inset-0` on the card div (line 111) with `h-full` to leverage CSS Grid's default `align-items: stretch` behavior — the card fills the grid cell naturally.
- Add `whitespace-nowrap` to the `<thead>` row so column headers never truncate/wrap, relying on the existing `overflow-x-auto` container for horizontal scroll when needed.

**Existing test patterns:** Tests in `test_recon_panel.py` render the partial via `client.get('/partials/recon')` and assert on HTML string content (class presence/absence). No existing tests reference the buggy classes (`lg:absolute`, `lg:inset-0`, `lg:relative`), so no tests will break. The existing `test_runs_table_overflow_container` (checks `overflow-x-auto`) and `test_grid_layout` (checks `grid-cols-1`, `lg:grid-cols-2`) remain valid.

steps.description: Write failing tests in TestReconRoute: (1) assert 'lg:absolute' NOT in rendered HTML, (2) assert 'lg:inset-0' NOT in rendered HTML, (3) assert right column card has 'h-full' class. All three fail against current template.

Fix recon.html lines 110-111: remove 'min-h-[400px] lg:relative' from wrapper div (make it plain '<div>'), replace 'lg:absolute lg:inset-0' on inner card div with 'h-full'. This makes the right column use natural CSS Grid stretch instead of absolute positioning.
Write failing test in TestReconRoute: assert the thead row contains 'whitespace-nowrap' class to prevent header text truncation. Fails against current template.

Fix recon.html line 117: add 'whitespace-nowrap' to the <tr> class in <thead> so column headers (especially 'When' and 'Detail') never truncate at narrow viewport widths. The existing overflow-x-auto container provides horizontal scrolling when needed.

design_decisions.decision: Use CSS Grid natural stretch (h-full) instead of absolute positioning
design_decisions.rationale: CSS Grid's default align-items:stretch makes grid cells match the tallest column. Adding h-full on the inner card fills the cell. This is simpler, avoids scroll/overlap issues, and matches the pattern used by other dashboard panels (performance.html, orchestrators.html). The absolute positioning pattern is not used anywhere else in the codebase.

design_decisions.decision: Remove min-h-[400px] from the wrapper div
design_decisions.rationale: The left column always contains 4 cards (Event Buffer, Burst State, Watermarks, Latest Verdict) which provide more than 400px of natural height. The min-height served no purpose beyond working around the absolute positioning layout; with natural grid flow it is unnecessary.

design_decisions.decision: Add whitespace-nowrap to thead tr rather than individual th elements or the table
design_decisions.rationale: Applying whitespace-nowrap to the header row prevents all header text from wrapping/truncating in one declaration. Not applied to the full table because data cells (e.g., trigger_reason) may benefit from wrapping at very narrow widths. The existing overflow-x-auto container provides horizontal scrolling as fallback.

reuse.how: Existing helper mocks all 6 recon data functions. New tests reuse this to render the partial with mock data, consistent with the 20+ existing tests in the file.
Standard Starlette TestClient fixture already used by all TestReconRoute tests. New tests use the same fixture.

reuse.what: Existing test pattern: HTML string assertion
reuse.how: Tests render the partial and check for CSS class presence/absence in the HTML string. New layout tests follow this exact pattern.
